findings: Deployment burst between 10:00 and 10:30: 4 BUYs filled in 52 seconds at 10:16:14-10:17:06 (M&M 25@3290, MARICO 60@820, ABCAPITAL 135@369, CHOLAHLDNG 25@1730) plus HFCL 600@133.5 still OPEN at 10:16:40. Pre-armed watch items for 11:00 tick: (a) if HFCL fills AND a 5th BUY is admitted by 11:00 → deployment likely > 78%, consider whether the cadence-quality complement (min_confidence 0.50 → 0.55) is warranted; (b) if BFSI cluster crosses 28% via a 5th BFSI name → consider max_sector_pct override 0.35 → 0.30 as back-pressure; (c) if any of today's 4 new fills marks below -3% on adverse drift → reassess SL discipline. actions_taken: HOLD — no changes. Overrides block remains empty. Override count 0 unchanged. Reason: (1) Nothing to loosen — at base on every lever, loosening floor reached. (2) Nothing to tighten — the deployment burst is legitimate strategy execution within all base-config bounds: BFSI 22.19% has 12.81 pp buffer to 35%, position max 8.22% has 11.78 pp buffer to 20%, deployment 65.50% has 14.50 pp buffer to 80%, daily loss -0.17% is 5.7% of the 3% limit. Tightening on a book that respects every base gate would be reactionary friction against the active-trader thesis. (3) The 4-BUY/52-sec cadence is the experiment running as designed; the right discipline is to log watch items (BFSI cluster trend, full-fill deployment, fresh-fill MTM drift) for the 11:00 tick rather than fire a speculative knob now.
